message:
feat(tantivy_reindex): Cx-first full_reindex + index_loop (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extend the tantivy Cx-first trail into reindex.rs. Adds the
Cx-first full-reindex entry point plus the shared checkpointed
index loop that future `reindex_range_with_cx` /
`backfill_with_cx` entry points will delegate to.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `ReindexPipeline::full_reindex_with_cx(cx, storage, config)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `full_reindex`. Checkpoints at three
    seams:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns before
        `create_event_reader`.
      - post-clear: a cancel after `writer.clear_all()` still
        surfaces `progress.docs_cleared` in the error context, so
        callers can see what was cleared before cancellation.
      - per-batch: via `index_loop_with_cx`.

  * `ReindexPipeline::index_loop_with_cx(cx, ...)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `index_loop`. Adds `cx.checkpoint()?` at the top
    of each batch iteration, embedding the current_ordinal and
    batches_committed counter in the error message. Per-record
    body is synchronous (CPU-only document mapping + writer ops,
    no await points) so the meaningful cancellation granularity
    is per-batch, matching the tick 52/53 pattern.

Deliberately kept narrow: `reindex_range`, `reindex_range_observed`,
`backfill`, and `index_loop_exclusive` are not migrated this tick.
They can follow in subsequent ticks using the same pattern — each
becomes a thin wrapper over `index_loop_with_cx` or its
exclusive-range parallel.

Regression test (new)
`full_reindex_with_cx_matches_legacy_cold_start`:

  * Two separate AppendLogRecorderStorage directories (legacy + cx)
    so their consumer checkpoints and event logs don't collide.
  * Same 5 events populated into each via `populate_log`.
  * Runs legacy `full_reindex` on one, Cx-first `full_reindex_with_cx`
    on the other.
  * Asserts ReindexProgress parity across ALL counter fields:
    events_read, events_indexed, events_skipped, events_filtered,
    caught_up, current_ordinal, batches_committed, docs_cleared.
  * Asserts `pipeline.writer().cleared == true` on the Cx-first
    side so the clear_before_start=true path actually fires.

If a future refactor of `index_loop_with_cx` accidentally skipped
the clear-all invocation or changed the per-batch commit logic,
the parity assertions would diverge from the legacy path.

Verification (local fork bypass, CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):
  * tantivy_reindex:: — 91 passed / 0 failed (tick 54: 90 -> tick
    55: 91, +1 new parity test; all 4 legacy full_reindex tests
    still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (31.46s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
